metadata.name: Cost Gate
metadata.description: Calculates production costs from the SSD and pricing configuration, presents a detailed cost breakdown to the user, and enforces budget thresholds. No billable work proceeds without passing the cost gate. This protocol ensures creators maintain control over their production budget at every stage.

triggers.trigger: Before any workflow step that incurs API/tool costs.
Before an entire workflow begins (aggregate estimate).
When the Director routes to a new workflow (cumulative check).

input.name: pricing.yaml
input.description: Tool and API pricing configuration. Contains per-unit costs for every tool used in the COOKIE production pipeline.

input.name: SSD (Scene Specification Document)
input.description: The scene specifications that define what assets need to be generated and at what quality level. Used to calculate quantities.

input.name: Execution Plan
input.description: The ordered execution plan with all tasks and their associated tools.

pricing_config.description: Expected structure of the pricing.yaml configuration file. This file maps tools to their per-unit costs at different quality levels.
pricing_config.expected_schema:
# pricing.yaml schema
version: "1.0"
# Budget configuration budget: per_episode_limit_usd: 50.00 # Maximum spend per episode per_scene_warn_usd: 5.00 # Warn when a single scene exceeds this per_scene_halt_usd: 10.00 # Halt when a single scene exceeds this cumulative_warn_percent: 80 # Warn at this % of per_episode_limit cumulative_halt_percent: 120 # Hard halt at this % of per_episode_limit retake_buffer_percent: 20 # Reserve this % of budget for retakes
# Per-tool pricing at different resolution/quality tiers tools: dall-e-3: description: "Image generation" unit: "image" tiers: draft: resolution: "1024x1024" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.040 standard: resolution: "1024x1024" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.040 hd: resolution: "1024x1792" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.080 4k: resolution: "1024x1792" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.080
runway-gen4: description: "Video generation" unit: "second" tiers: draft: resolution: "720p" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.050 standard: resolution: "1080p" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.050 hd: resolution: "1080p" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.050 4k: resolution: "4K" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.100
elevenlabs: description: "Voice narration" unit: "character" tiers: draft: quality: "standard" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.00018 standard: quality: "standard" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.00018 hd: quality: "professional" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.00030 4k: quality: "professional" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.00030
suno: description: "Music generation" unit: "track" tiers: draft: duration: "30s" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.10 standard: duration: "60s" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.10 hd: duration: "120s" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.20 4k: duration: "120s" cost_per_unit_usd: 0.20
gpt-4o: description: "Text generation (LLM)" unit: "1k_tokens" tiers: draft: cost_per_unit_usd: 0.005 standard: cost_per_unit_usd: 0.005 hd: cost_per_unit_usd: 0.005 4k: cost_per_unit_usd: 0.005

cost_calculation.description: How the cost gate calculates estimated costs from the SSD and pricing configuration.

step.name: Load Pricing Configuration
step.action: Read `{project-root}/_cookie/_config/pricing.yaml` using the discover-inputs protocol with FULL_LOAD strategy. Validate it against the expected schema. If missing or invalid, trigger `on_error.missing_file` or `on_error.validation_failure`.

step.name: Determine Quality Tier
step.action: Read the resolution/quality setting from the workflow context or episode config. This determines which pricing tier to use for all cost calculations. Supported tiers: draft, standard, hd, 4k. Default to "standard" if not specified.

step.name: Parse SSD for Asset Quantities
step.action:
Analyze the SSD to determine what needs to be generated:
Count of first-frames and character reference sheets. Derived from the number of scenes and unique characters.
Total video duration across all scenes. Derived from scene durations specified in the SSD.
Total character count for narration text. Derived from dialogue and narration scripts in the SSD.
Number of music tracks needed. Derived from the episode music requirements in the SSD.
Number of sound effects needed. Derived from scene-level SFX annotations in the SSD.
Estimated LLM token usage for prompt generation, script refinement, and other text tasks.

step.name: Calculate Per-Scene Costs
step.action: For each scene in the SSD: - Identify all assets required for that scene. - Look up the per-unit cost for each tool at the selected tier. - Multiply quantity by per-unit cost. - Sum to get the total estimated cost for the scene. - Apply the retake buffer (add retake_buffer_percent to the estimate).

step.name: Calculate Per-Tool Costs
step.action: Aggregate costs across all scenes, grouped by tool. This shows which tools are the biggest cost drivers.

step.name: Calculate Total Estimated Cost
step.action: Sum all per-scene costs to get the total workflow cost. Add the retake buffer as a separate line item. Calculate the cumulative cost (this workflow + all previous workflows in the session).

step.name: Calculate Resolution Alternatives
step.action: Re-run the cost calculation for each available quality tier (draft, standard, hd, 4k) to show the user cost comparisons. This helps users make informed decisions about quality vs. budget.

cost_presentation.description: Format for presenting the cost breakdown to the user before requesting approval.

thresholds.description: Budget thresholds that trigger warnings or halts during cost evaluation. These protect against unexpectedly high costs.

threshold.description: Warn when a single scene's estimated cost exceeds this amount.
threshold.action: Display a warning to the user highlighting the expensive scene. Show which tools are driving the high cost. Suggest alternatives (lower resolution, fewer retakes, simpler scene). Allow the user to approve or modify.

threshold.description: Halt when a single scene's estimated cost exceeds this amount.
threshold.action: HALT the workflow. Do not proceed without explicit user intervention. Display the cost breakdown for the expensive scene. Require the user to either: - Approve the cost explicitly. - Choose a lower quality tier. - Modify the scene specification to reduce cost. - Skip the scene. Trigger `on_error.cost_exceeded` from error-recovery.xml.

threshold.description: Warn when cumulative session cost reaches this percentage of the per-episode budget limit.
threshold.action: Display a budget utilization warning. Show how much budget remains. Show estimated cost of remaining work. Advise the user whether the remaining budget is sufficient to complete the episode.

threshold.description: Hard halt when cumulative session cost reaches this percentage of the per-episode budget limit.
threshold.action: HALT the entire production session. No further billable work is permitted until the user explicitly increases the budget or resets the session cost tracker. Trigger `on_error.cost_exceeded` from error-recovery.xml. Present a full cost report of all workflows in the session.

approval_flow.description: The approval flow that must be completed before billable work begins.

standard_mode.description: Normal operation mode. User sees the cost estimate and must explicitly approve before work begins.

procedure.step: Check thresholds (Section 4). If any halt threshold is hit, stop here.

Prompt the user for approval:
Approve and proceed with the estimated cost. Approve but switch to a different quality tier. Go back and modify the SSD to reduce cost. Skip this workflow entirely. Cancel the entire production session.
If approved: record the approval in the workflow context and proceed. If not approved: halt the workflow with the user's chosen action.

yolo_mode.description: "YOLO" mode allows faster iteration by auto-approving costs that fall within thresholds. However, the cost estimate is still presented and approval is still required. YOLO mode simply reduces the friction — it auto-approves if costs are within normal bounds and only asks for manual approval on threshold violations.

procedure.step: If ALL thresholds pass (no warnings, no halts): - Display the total estimated cost on a single line. - STILL REQUIRE explicit user approval (brief confirmation). - Rationale: even in yolo mode, the user must acknowledge costs.
If any threshold is violated: - Fall back to standard_mode behavior. - Show the full cost breakdown and require manual decision.

yolo_uncapped_mode.description: "YOLO Uncapped" mode bypasses all approval gates. Costs are still calculated and logged, but the user is NOT prompted for approval. This mode is for experienced users who want zero interruptions. USE WITH CAUTION — there is no spending limit enforcement.

procedure.step: Log the cost estimate to the workflow cost tracker.
Display a brief one-line cost notification (non-blocking): "[cost-gate] Estimated: $X.XX | Session total: $Y.YY | Proceeding..."
Proceed immediately without waiting for approval. Thresholds are still checked but only logged as warnings — they do not halt execution.

yolo_uncapped_mode.safety_net: Even in yolo-uncapped mode, a hard safety limit is enforced: If cumulative session cost exceeds 5x the per_episode_limit, the workflow HALTS regardless of mode. This prevents catastrophic runaway costs due to bugs or infinite loops. 5

cost_tracking.description: How actual costs are tracked during and after workflow execution, compared against estimates, and persisted for reporting.

during_execution.description: As each step executes, actual costs are recorded and compared against the pre-approved estimates.

rules.rule: After each billable step completes, record the actual cost reported by the tool/API in the workflow cost tracker.
Compare actual cost against the estimated cost for that step. If actual exceeds estimate by more than 50%, log a warning.
Maintain a running total of actual costs. Re-check cumulative thresholds after each step to catch budget overruns mid-workflow.
If the running actual cost exceeds the approved estimate by more than the retake buffer percentage, halt the workflow and re-present the cost gate with updated actuals.

post_execution.description: After workflow completion, produce a cost reconciliation report comparing estimates to actuals.

report_fields.field: Side-by-side estimate vs. actual per step.
How much of the retake buffer was used.

on_error.description: Triggered when any cost threshold is exceeded (per-scene halt or cumulative halt).
on_error.action: Delegate to error-recovery.xml `cost_exceeded` handler: - Halt the workflow. - Present the full cost report. - Ask the user for a decision: increase budget, lower quality, modify scope, or cancel.

on_error.description: Triggered when pricing.yaml or SSD files are missing.
on_error.action: Delegate to error-recovery.xml `missing_file` handler. Cost calculation cannot proceed without pricing data.

on_error.description: Triggered when pricing.yaml has invalid format or missing required fields.
on_error.action: Delegate to error-recovery.xml `validation_failure` handler. Report which fields are missing or invalid.
